raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-directed reframing to equalize perceived significance of temporal extremes",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "duration does not alter final experiential outcome",
    "long and short intervals share an identical terminal effect",
    "subjective weighting of time length is irrelevant to result"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent under a teleological view of time but collapses if duration itself constitutes part of the effect",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "patient experiences distress specifically from overvaluing temporal length rather than from objective differences in duration or intensity",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "actual differences in duration produce materially different psychological, physiological or behavioral sequelae that the statement dismisses"
}
